title: Intensely Peppery Argentinian Beef Empanadas
description: A traditional Argentinian dish featuring a peppery beef filling inside a crumbly pastry, perfect for a snack or light meal. This recipe is egg-free and uses a mixture powder for added flavor.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the ground beef, mixture powder, black pepper, onion powder, and salt, mixing until just combined.
In a separate large bowl, whisk together the flour and cold unsalted butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add the water to the flour mixture, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together in a ball.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 5 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each into a thin circle.
Place 1/4 cup of the beef mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling, forming a half-moon shape, and press the edges together to seal.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little water and cut a small slit in the top of each to allow steam to escape.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and broil for 2-3 minutes, until golden brown.
